findings: --teardown is best-effort only: it sends one kill to the recorded PID, does not wait or escalate, does not kill descendants/process groups, then deletes pid files and kills tmux, so stuck child processes can survive cleanup
duplicate-session restart only kills the tmux session and relaunches; it does not invoke teardown or reclaim stale claimed work after crash/relaunch
failure handling is asymmetric: commander launch failure records run-state failure, but ordinary worker failure lacks comparable state/result marker or task recovery in this script
teardown updates phase to torn_down but does not set a terminal status, leaving room for stale monitor semantics after teardown
